sections.label: Section 1.170
sections.translation: of far-famed Arethusa near the sea , so that I might behold the army of the Achaeans and the ships rowed by those godlike heroes ; for our husbands tell us

sections.label: Section 1.175
sections.translation: that fair-haired Menelaus and high-born Agamemnon are leading them to Troy on a thousand ships in quest of Helen , whom Paris the herdsman

sections.label: Section 1.180
sections.translation: carried off from the banks of reedy Eurotas , his gift from Aphrodite , when that queen of Cyprus entered beauty ’ s contest with Hera and Pallas at the gushing fountain . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.185
sections.translation: Through the grove of Artemis , rich with sacrifice , I sped my course , my cheek stained with red from maiden modesty , in my eagerness to see the soldiers ’ camp ,

sections.label: Section 1.190
sections.translation: the tents of the mail-clad Danaids , and their crowd of horses . [*] I saw two met together in council ; one was Aias , son of Oileus ; the other Aias , son of Telamon , crown of glory to the men of Salamis ;

sections.label: Section 1.195
sections.translation: and I saw Protesilaus and Palamedes , sprung from the son of Poseidon , sitting there amusing themselves with intricate figures at checkers ; Diomedes too

sections.label: Section 1.200
sections.translation: at his favorite sport of hurling quoits ; and Meriones , Ares ’ son , a marvel to mankind , stood at his side ; likewise I beheld the son of Laertes , who came from his island hills , and with him Nireus ,

sections.label: Section 1.205
sections.translation: handsomest of the Achaeans .

sections.label: Section 206
sections.translation: Achilles next , that nimble runner , swift on his feet as the wind , whom Thetis bore and Chiron trained , I saw

sections.label: Section 210
sections.translation: upon the beach , racing in full armor along the shingle , and straining every nerve to beat a team of four horses ,

sections.label: Section 215
sections.translation: as he sped round the track on foot ; and Eumelus , the grandson of Pheres , their driver , was shouting when I saw him , goading on his lovely steeds ,

sections.label: Section 220
sections.translation: with their bits of chased gold-work ; the center pair , that bore the yoke , had dappled coats picked out with white , while the tracehorses , on the outside , facing the turning-post in the course , [*]

sections.label: Section 225
sections.translation: were bays with spotted fetlocks . Close beside them Peleus ’ son leapt on his way , in all his harness , keeping abreast

sections.label: Section 230
sections.translation: the rail by the axle-box .

sections.label: Section 2.231
sections.translation: Next I sought the countless fleet , a wonder to behold , that I might fill my girlish eyes with gazing , a sweet [*] delight .

sections.label: Section 2.235
sections.translation: The warlike Myrmidons from Phthia held the right wing with fifty swift cruisers , upon whose sterns , right at the ends , stood Nereid goddesses

sections.label: Section 2.240
sections.translation: in golden effigy , the ensign of Achilles ’ armament .

sections.label: Section 2.242
sections.translation: Near these were moored the Argive ships in equal numbers , over which Mecisteus ’

sections.label: Section 2.245
sections.original: παῖς ἦν , Ταλαὸς ὃν τρέφει πατήρ ,
sections.translation: son , whom Talaus his grandfather reared , and Sthenelus , son of Capaneus , were in command ; next in order , Theseus ’ son was stationed at the head of sixty ships from Attica , having the goddess

sections.label: Section 2.250
sections.translation: Pallas set in a winged chariot drawn by steeds with solid hoof , a lucky sight for mariners .

sections.label: Section 3.253
sections.translation: Then I saw Boeotia ’ s fleet of fifty sails

sections.label: Section 3.255
sections.translation: decked with ensigns ; these had Cadmus at the stern holding a golden dragon at the beaks of the vessels , and earth-born Leitus

sections.label: Section 3.260
sections.translation: was their admiral . And there were ships from Phocis ; and from Locris came the son of Oileus with an equal contingent , leaving famed Thronium ’ s citadel .

sections.label: Section 3.265
sections.translation: And from Mycenae , the Cyclopes ’ town , Atreus ’ son sent a hundred well-manned galleys , and [*] was with him in command , as friend with friend ,

sections.label: Section 3.270
sections.translation: that Hellas might exact vengeance on the one who had fled her home to wed a foreigner . Also I saw upon Gerenian Nestor ’ s prows from Pylos

sections.label: Section 3.275
sections.translation: the ensign of his neighbour Alpheus , four-footed like a bull .

sections.label: Section 4.277
sections.translation: Moreover there was a squadron of twelve Aenianian sail under King Gouneus ; and then near them

sections.label: Section 4.280
sections.translation: the lords of Elis , whom all the people named Epeians ; and Eurytus was lord of these ; Iikewise he led the Taphian warriors with the white oar-blades , the subjects of Meges ,

sections.label: Section 4.285
sections.translation: son of Phyleus , who had left the isles of the Echinades , where sailors cannot land .

sections.label: Section 4.288
sections.translation: Lastly , Aias , reared in Salamis ,

sections.label: Section 4.290
sections.original: δεξιὸν κέρας πρὸς τὸ λαιὸν ξυνᾶγε ,
sections.translation: was joining his right wing to the left [*] of those near whom he was posed , closing the line with his outermost ships , twelve barques obedient to the helm , as I heard and then

sections.label: Section 4.295
sections.translation: saw the crews ; the one who brings his barbaric boats to grapple Aias shall obtain no safe return . There I saw [*]

sections.label: Section 4.300
sections.translation: the naval armament , but some things I heard at home about the gathered army , of which I still have a recollection .

sections.label: Section 303
sections.original: Μενέλαε , τολμᾷς δείνʼ , ἅ σʼ οὐ τολμᾶν χρεών .
sections.translation: Strange daring yours , Menelaus , where you have no right .

sections.label: Section 304
sections.translation: Stand back ! You carry loyalty to your master too far .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.translation: The very reproach you have for me is to my credit .

sections.label: Section 306
sections.original: κλαίοις ἄν , εἰ πράσσοις ἃ μὴ πράσσειν σε δεῖ .
sections.translation: You shall rue it , if you meddle in matters that do not concern you .

sections.label: Section 307
sections.original: οὐ χρῆν σε λῦσαι δέλτον , ἣν ἐγὼ ʼφερον .
sections.translation: You had no right to open a letter , which I was carrying .

sections.label: Section 308
sections.original: οὐδέ γε φέρειν σὲ πᾶσιν Ἕλλησιν κακά .
sections.translation: No , nor you to be carrying sorrow to all Hellas .

sections.label: Section 309
sections.original: ἄλλοις ἁμιλλῶ ταῦτʼ · ἄφες δὲ τήνδʼ ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Argue that point with others , but surrender that letter to me .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.translation: I shall not let go .

sections.label: Section 310b
sections.translation: Nor will I let loose my hold .

sections.label: Section 311
sections.original: σκήπτρῳ τάχʼ ἆρα σὸν καθαιμάξω κάρα .
sections.translation: Why then , this staff of mine will be dabbling your head with blood before long .

sections.label: Section 312
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐκλεές τοι δεσποτῶν θνῄσκειν ὕπερ .
sections.translation: To die in my master ’ s cause would be a noble death .

sections.label: Section 313
sections.original: μέθες · μακροὺς δὲ δοῦλος ὢν λέγεις λόγους .
sections.translation: Let go ! you are too wordy for a slave .

sections.label: Section 314
sections.original: ὦ δέσποτʼ , ἀδικούμεσθα . σὰς δʼ ἐπιστολὰς
sections.translation: Master , he is wronging me ; he snatched

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: ἐξαρπάσας ὅδʼ ἐκ χερῶν ἐμῶν βίᾳ ,
sections.translation: your letter violently from my grasp , Agamemnon , and will not heed the claims of right .

sections.label: Section 317
sections.translation: Ah ! what is this uproar at the gates , this indecent brawling ?

sections.label: Section 318
sections.original: οὑμὸς οὐχ ὁ τοῦδε μῦθος κυριώτερος λέγειν .
sections.translation: My tale , not his , has the better right to be spoken .

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: σὺ δὲ τί τῷδʼ ἐς ἔριν ἀφῖξαι , Μενέλεως , βίᾳ τʼ ἄγεις ;
sections.translation: You , Menelaus ! what quarrel do you have with this man , why are you dragging him here ? Exit ATTENDANT .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: βλέψον εἰς ἡμᾶς , ἵνʼ ἀρχὰς τῶν λόγων ταύτας λάβω .
sections.translation: Look me in the face ! May that be the prelude to my story .

sections.label: Section 321
sections.original: μῶν τρέσας οὐκ ἀνακαλύψω βλέφαρον , Ἀτρέως γεγώς ;
sections.translation: Shall I , the son of Atreus , close my eyes from fear ? [*]

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: τήνδʼ ὁρᾷς δέλτον , κακίστων γραμμάτων ὑπηρέτιν ;
sections.translation: Do you see this tablet , the bearer of a shameful message ?

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: εἰσορῶ · καὶ πρῶτα ταύτην σῶν ἀπάλλαξον χερῶν .
sections.translation: I see it , yes ; now , you first of all surrender it .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: οὔ , πρὶν ἂν δείξω γε Δαναοῖς πᾶσι τἀγγεγραμμένα .
sections.translation: No , not till I have shown its contents to all the army .

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ οἶσθʼ ἃ μή σε καιρὸς εἰδέναι σήμαντρʼ ἀνείς ;
sections.translation: What ! have you broken the seal and know already what you should never have known ?

sections.label: Section 326
sections.original: ὥστε σʼ ἀλγῦναί γʼ , ἀνοίξας ἃ σὺ κάκʼ εἰργάσω λάθρᾳ .
sections.translation: Yes , I opened it and know to your sorrow the secret machinations of your heart .

sections.label: Section 327
sections.original: ποῦ δὲ κἄλαβές νιν ; ὦ θεοί , σῆς ἀναισχύντου φρενός .
sections.translation: Where did you get it ? O gods ! what shameless heart you have !

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: προσδοκῶν σὴν παῖδʼ ἀπʼ Ἄργους , εἰ στράτευμʼ ἀφίξεται .
sections.translation: I was awaiting your daughter ’ s arrival at the camp in Argos .

sections.label: Section 329
sections.original: τί δέ σε τἀμὰ δεῖ φυλάσσειν ; οὐκ ἀναισχύντου τόδε ;
sections.translation: What right have you to watch my doings ? is not this a proof of shamelessness ?
